faqs.question: How do I know if I have carpenter ants versus regular ants in my Forest Hills home?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants are larger than common ants (typically ¼ to ½ inch long) and are usually black or dark brown. Signs include piles of wood shavings (frass) near wooden structures, rustling sounds in walls, and winged ants appearing indoors in spring. Our technicians can positively identify the species during a free inspection.

faqs.question: Are carpenter ants common in Forest Hills, Queens?
faqs.answer: Yes, carpenter ants are very common in Forest Hills due to the mature trees, wooden structures, and moisture from the area's lush landscaping. The neighborhood's proximity to Forest Park and abundance of older homes with wooden components make it particularly susceptible to carpenter ant activity, especially from spring through fall.
